nativeRange.habitat: Shallow coastal reef habitats; typically found attached to hard substrates such as rock, rubble, and dead coral in the photic zone

avoidWith: herbivorous fish likely to graze heavily on macroalgae

summary: A genus-level entry consolidating 11 Mosaic Macros Laurencia sp. trade forms — compact, knobby-branching red macroalgae in purple, pink, rust, and yellow color variants suited to display reef tanks.
careNotes:
Flat Purple Laurencia is a genus-level entry (Laurencia sp., family Rhodomelaceae, order Ceramiales) consolidating 11 Mosaic Macros trade variants that could not be resolved to individual species: Flat Purple, Cherry Tree, Pink Succulent, Pink Knobby, Purple Intricate, Purple Knobby, Purple Asparagus, Encrusting, Rusty, Corky Brown, and Yellow Laurencia. All share a characteristic Laurencia growth form — compact, repeatedly branching thalli with club-shaped or knobby branch tips — and vary primarily in coloration and subtle morphology. Mosaic is currently the sole specialty hobby source for most of these variants.

As with the sibling entry Color-Shifting Laurencia (sw-algae-030, Laurencia iridescens / Yuzurua iridescens), all Laurencia sp. variants in this entry are red algae maintained under standard reef parameters: temperature generally advised around 24–27°C, salinity around 1.023–1.026 SG, and pH 7.8–8.4. Moderate lighting (medium intensity) tends to be recommended for ornamental red macroalgae in family Rhodomelaceae. Mosaic product descriptions characterize these as fairly slow-growing; the manifest specifies medium growth rate at genus level, reflecting the broader Laurencia genus which spans slow-to-medium growth. Nutrient uptake is generally considered low — these are kept as ornamental display specimens rather than refugium nutrient exporters.

All variants have been documented as suitable for attachment to live rock, rubble, or hard substrate in the display tank. Secure anchoring of the holdfast tends to improve long-term success. Good water flow has been reported to benefit health and prevent detritus accumulation on the thallus. Propagation in the aquarium is typically by fragmentation. Herbivorous fish (tangs, rabbitfish, large urchins) may graze on Laurencia; isolation from heavy grazers is generally advisable for ornamental display use.

The Encrusting Laurencia variant deviates morphologically from the typical upright branching form — it may represent a different genus or growth stage; the Mosaic listing identifies it as Laurencia sp. nonetheless. The Rusty Laurencia and Corky Brown Laurencia variants may overlap with material sold elsewhere as Laurencia obtusa or related brown-russet species; no species-level identification was confirmed. Per Mosaic care guide guidance: as a red or brown alga, this species will MELT at the base if buried in sand — attach to rock via superglue, zip-tie/fishing line, or wedging instead. (Sand-bed acceptance per Mosaic is limited to Caulerpa, Halimeda, Penicillus, Rhipocephalus, and Udotea genera, plus some calcified Dasycladales.)

primary.name: Mosaic Macros
primary.notes: Primary and sole specialty hobby source for this genus-level consolidated entry. Product page confirms Laurencia sp. identification, small branching morphology, and general compatibility notes. Detailed water parameters not stated; extrapolated from genus-level and family-level (Rhodomelaceae) knowledge cross-referenced with sibling species entry sw-algae-030.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros – Cherry Tree Laurencia
additional.notes: Secondary Mosaic listing for consolidated variant. Confirms stubby branching morphology with knobby tips; no additional parameters.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros – Purple Intricate Laurencia
additional.notes: Secondary Mosaic listing for consolidated variant. Confirms small branching morphology; no additional parameters.

additional.name: AquaMate – Color-Shifting Laurencia (sw-algae-030)
additional.notes: Sibling Laurencia entry (Yuzurua iridescens, family Rhodomelaceae, order Ceramiales) used as cross-reference for genus-level water parameters, care level, and placement conventions. Parameters from that researched entry applied here by family-level extrapolation.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros general macroalgae care guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source — provides authoritative Mosaic-wide parameter targets (temp 22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, Ca 400-450 ppm, Mg 1250-1350 ppm, NO3 5-20 ppm, PO4 0.02-0.1 ppm) and the 4-method propagation standard. Added retroactively 2026-06-08.
